scheme_name: PMMSY: Ice Plant/Cold Store of Minimum 30-Ton Capacity - Haryana
page_title: PMMSY: Ice Plant/Cold Store of Minimum 30-Ton Capacity - Haryana

details: The Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Ice Plant/Cold Store of minimum 30-ton capacity, introduced by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to provide the subsidy to support the establishment of ice plants or cold storage facilities with a minimum capacity of 30 tons to strengthen fish preservation and cold chain infrastructure in Haryana.

This centrally sponsored scheme is jointly funded 50% by the Central Government and 50% by the State Government. Beneficiaries must submit an undertaking in their DPR/SCP to keep the unit operational, bear all post-construction costs, and ensure ice is supplied to fishers and fish farmers at affordable prices.
benefits: A 40% subsidy is available for the General Category.
A 60% subsidy is offered for SC (Scheduled Caste) and Women beneficiaries.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Haryana and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).

The applicant must submit an undertaking in the DPR/SCP to bear all operational, maintenance, and post-construction management costs, and to ensure the continuous operation of the ice plant/cold storage.
The applicant must note that central assistance will be provided based on the actual cost, within the overall ceiling specified under the relevant PMMSY sub-components.
The applicant must ensure that the ice produced is supplied to fishers and fish farmers at an affordable price.
The applicant must either own the project land or hold it on a long-term registered lease of at least 10 years to be eligible.

online: For registration, click on “New User/Register Here” and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number & password. Click on ‘Submit’.
Verify the OTP received in the provided Email and Mobile Number. Alternatively, click on the Verification Link to activate the profile.
Login to Apply for the Scheme:
The “Sign in here” option is available on the right side of the screen. Fill in the required credentials and click on the ‘Login’ button.
In the opened window, click on “Scheme/Services list”, and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
Now, select the scheme and click on “Apply for Service/Scheme”.
Fill in all the details in the online application form and click on "Submit".
Type SARAL and send to 9954699899 to track your application.
Type SARAL<space><Application ID/Ticket No.> and send to 9954699899 to track your application/ticket.

documents_required: Date of Birth Certificate (Voter ID/Birth Certificate/Aadhaar/Matriculation Certificate)
Caste Certificate from First Class Magistrate (Tehsildar/SDM)
Agreement Deed between beneficiary and Fisheries Department
Training Certificate related to the operation/maintenance of cold storage
Land Record from Tehsil (Jamabandi, Nakal Akshizra, Fard, Registered Patta Nama) with clear title and latest records
Photographs of Cold Storage with beneficiary
Detail of Bank Account and PAN Card
Detailed Project Report (DPR) or Self-Contained Proposal (SCP) as per PMMSY guidelines
All bills above ₹50,000 as per E-way/Income Tax/GST rules
Certificate from C.A. with the bills certifying payment compliance with Income Tax/GST rules
NOC from Gram Sabha/Municipal Corporation/Authority of the area where the unit is located
Clear Site Plan/Area Site Plan attached with subsidy case
All documents should be in readable form

faqs.question: How can I apply for the Ice Plant/Cold Storage subsidy under PMMSY?
faqs.answer: To apply, you must have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) and submit a Detailed Project Report (DPR) or Self-Contained Proposal (SCP) along with all required documents through Antyodaya-SARAL.

faqs.question: Is it necessary to own land to be eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, land on long-term registered lease is also acceptable, provided the lease is for a minimum of 10 years and has a clear title.

faqs.question: Is there any specific requirement regarding the price of ice sold from the plant?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must ensure that the ice produced is supplied to fishers and fish farmers at an affordable price.

faqs.question: What is the time limit for getting a response after applying?
faqs.answer: The Right to Service (RTS) time limit is 50 days from the date of application submission.

faqs.question: What type of agreement needs to be submitted with the application?
faqs.answer: An agreement deed must be submitted between the beneficiary and the Fisheries Department as part of the required documentation.

faqs.question: Is any training certificate required for applying?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must submit a training certificate related to the operation and maintenance of cold storage facilities.

faqs.question: What kind of photographs do I need to submit?
faqs.answer: You must submit photographs of the cold storage along with yourself (the beneficiary) in the frame.

faqs.question: What is the service fee?
faqs.answer: No Govt fee; Rs. 10 Service Charge; Rs. 10 Atal Seva Kendra Service Charge.

faqs.question: Do all documents need to be in any specific format?
faqs.answer: Yes, all documents submitted must be in readable form to be considered valid for the subsidy case.

faqs.question: What email or helpline can I contact for further assistance?
faqs.answer: You can contact the helpline at 0172-3968400 or email saral.haryana@gov.in for any queries related to the application.
